verification_performed: Confirmed the task is ACTIVE on the board
Confirmed the brief and handoff are present in 05_INBOX_FROM_MANAGER
Confirmed this remains paper-only / observation-mode rebuild work
Used completed rebuild audits to anchor storage/schema decisions

Wrote a concrete implementation-ready spec artifact defining canonical storage, raw vs normalized separation, required entities, field tiers, partitioning, versioning, completeness, and updater idempotency rules

raw_vs_normalized.raw: minimally transformed source-of-truth MLB Stats API records
raw_vs_normalized.normalized: rebuild-ready standardized records derived only from raw

foundation_spec_summary.completeness_definition: complete season-to-date coverage means every completed 2026 MLB game through the target coverage date exists in raw games plus matching team logs, pitcher logs, bullpen context where derivable, and game_state_history where available, with manifests recording any source-unavailable gaps explicitly
foundation_spec_summary.daily_updater_rule: future updater must fetch previous-day completed games only, rebuild the full date partition, validate counts, atomically replace the target partition, and write a manifest; no duplicate game_pk/entity rows allowed

required_fields_cover: inning / half / outs / score-state progression where available
optional_high_value_entities_or_fields: advanced derived context not required for first-pass backfill

storage_schema_summary.normalized_format: jsonl or parquet if later needed, but raw stays append-readable
storage_schema_summary.versioning: foundation dataset version v1 with explicit metadata and no silent schema mutation

recommended_next_enabled_task: MLB_CURRENT_SEASON_BACKFILL_BUILD_001. The spec now defines a concrete canonical target, so the season-to-date backfill can open against a stable raw/normalized storage contract instead of guessing.
